heading: Review of African elephant conservation programs
label: 4221 Review of African elephant conservation programs

children.text: publishing a notice in the Federal Register requesting submission of such information to the Secretary by all interested parties; and
submitting a written request for such information through the Secretary of State to each ivory producing country.

children.label: A
children.text: The country is a party to CITES and adheres to the CITES Ivory Control System.

children.label: B
children.text: The country’s elephant conservation program is based on the best available information, and the country is making expeditious progress in compiling information on the elephant habitat condition and carrying capacity, total population and population trends, and the annual reproduction and mortality of the elephant populations within the country.

children.label: C
children.text: The taking of elephants in the country is effectively controlled and monitored.

children.label: D
children.text: The country’s ivory quota is determined on the basis of information referred to in subparagraph (B) and reflects the amount of ivory which is confiscated or consumed domestically by the country.

children.label: E
children.text: The country has not authorized or allowed the export of amounts of raw ivory which exceed its ivory quota under the CITES Ivory Control System.

children.heading: Delay in issuing determination
children.label: 2 Delay in issuing determination
children.text: If the Secretary finds within one year after , that there is insufficient information upon which to make the determination under paragraph (1), the Secretary may delay issuing the determination until no later than . The Secretary shall issue and publish in the Federal Register at the time of the finding a statement explaining the reasons for any such delay.October 7, 1988December 31, 1989
